source_old_req: The mitigationActor_th thread shall operate with a periodic dispatch protocol, a period of 3000 milliseconds, a deadline of 500 milliseconds, and a priority of 8. The thread shall accept a valid input errorData value on the mitigation_in event data port and produce a corresponding actionData value on the mitigation_command event data port. Environmental assumptions shall specify that the mitigation_in port receives valid errorData values. The thread shall guarantee that the mitigation_command port output is determined exclusively by the current mitigation_in input and internal computations performed by the mitigationActor subprogram. The thread shall further guarantee that all state updates maintain the integrity of the data types associated with the mitigation_in and mitigation_command ports.
natural_requirement: The mitigationActor_th component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for mitigationActor_th.impl. In the implementation layer, mitigationActor_th.impl shall route data flow from mitigation_in to mitigationActor.inData_para through C1 and control signal from mitigationActor.outData_para to mitigation_command through C2.

requirement_items.text: The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall route the data flow from mitigation_in to mitigationActor.inData_para through connection C1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from mitigation_in to mitigationActor.inData_para.

requirement_items.text: The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall route the control signal from mitigationActor.outData_para to mitigation_command through connection C2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from mitigationActor.outData_para to mitigation_command.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from mitigation_in to mitigationActor.inData_para. The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from mitigationActor.outData_para to mitigation_command.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall route the data flow from mitigation_in to mitigationActor.inData_para through connection C1. The mitigationActor_th.impl implementation shall route the control signal from mitigationActor.outData_para to mitigation_command through connection C2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
